title: Malvastrum asperrimum
intro: Malvastrum asperrimum, also known as the rough-leaved mallow, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Malvaceae, native to the Americas. The health benefits of Malvastrum asperrimum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Malvastrum asperrimum is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Malvastrum asperrimum is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Malvastrum, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The plant is a part of the larger Malvaceae family, which includes other plants like hibiscus and okra. The classification of Malvastrum asperrimum is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, family - Malvaceae, genus - Malvastrum, species - asperrimum. The exact classification of the plant can be found in various botanical databases and sources.
